name: Biscochitos
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. In a medium bowl, combine flour, anise seed, baking powder, and salt. Set aside.
3. Beat shortening in a large bowl of electric mixer on medium speed until creamy. Gradually add half the sugar; beat until fluffy.
4. Blend in egg. Gradually add flour mixture alternately with orange juice, mixing well after each addition.
5. Divide dough into two portions. Roll out one portion at a time on a lightly floured surface to 1/4 inch thickness. Cover remaining dough to prevent drying.
6. Cut out cookies using fancy cookie cutters, 2 to 2 1/2 inches in diameter. While cutting cookies, add scraps to the remaining dough.
7. If the dough becomes too soft to handle, refrigerate briefly.
8. Place cookies 1 inch apart on an ungreased baking sheet. Prepare cinnamon topping by combining the remaining sugar and cinnamon; lightly sprinkle over cookies.
9. Bake for 8 to 10 minutes or until edges are lightly browned. Cool on racks, then store in an airtight container.
10. Makes 4 to 5 dozen cookies.
